summary: Tested NEW Norma Router and Vendor API features. All 30 backend API tests passed (100%). Frontend UI tests passed - thermal calculator frame dropdown correctly shows vendor profiles with [Vendor] prefix. Norma Router correctly maps 10 product types to standards (EN 13241, EN 14351-1, EN 1090-1) with mandatory fields. Vendor API multi-key system works: key creation, catalog import (NF-Standard JSON), upsert, merged thermal-profiles, and key revocation all verified.

norma_router.GET /api/certificazioni/router/product-t: PASS - Returns 10 product types with id, label, standards, has_thermal
norma_router.GET /api/certificazioni/router/cancello: PASS - Returns EN 13241 with mandatory: product_type, mechanical_resistance, safe_opening, durability
norma_router.GET /api/certificazioni/router/finestra: PASS - Returns EN 14351-1 with ThermalValidator required, thermal_uw mandatory
norma_router.GET /api/certificazioni/router/tettoia: PASS - Returns EN 1090-1 with execution_class mandatory
norma_router.Router public (no auth): PASS - Router endpoints accessible without authentication

vendor_api.POST /api/vendor/keys: PASS - Creates vendor key with nf_vk_ prefix
vendor_api.GET /api/vendor/keys: PASS - Lists keys with api_key_masked field
vendor_api.POST /api/vendor/import_catalog (upsert): PASS - Same vendor+system updates existing catalog
vendor_api.GET /api/vendor/catalogs/{catalog_id}: PASS - Returns single catalog with profiles
vendor_api.GET /api/vendor/thermal-profiles: PASS - Returns merged frames (8 builtin + vendor profiles with source='vendor')

frontend.Thermal calculator frame dropdown: PASS - Shows [Vendor] prefix for vendor profiles
frontend.Uw calculation: PASS - Calculates correctly with zone compliance badges

critical_code_review_comments: NormaRouter in router.py correctly implements ProductType enum with 10 types and ROUTING_TABLE mapping
vendor_api.py uses verify_vendor_key dependency with X-Vendor-Key header middleware properly
Upsert logic in import_catalog correctly checks vendor+system combination before insert/update
Merged thermal-profiles correctly combines builtin FRAME_TYPES + vendor profiles (with uf) + custom user profiles
Frontend CertificazioneWizardPage.js properly fetches /vendor/thermal-profiles and merges with reference data
Frontend displays [Vendor] prefix for source='vendor' profiles in dropdown
test_credentials: Created temp test users via MongoDB (vendor_session_*, frontend_vendor_session_*, router_vendor_session_*), all cleaned up after tests
seed_data_creation: None - all test data cleaned up
context_for_next_testing_agent: Norma Router + Vendor API fully tested. Router maps: cancello/portone -> EN 13241, finestra/portafinestra -> EN 14351-1 (thermal required), tettoia/scala/soppalco/ringhiera/pensilina/recinzione -> EN 1090-1 (structural). Vendor API uses X-Vendor-Key header (not Bearer auth). Keys are per-user (owner_id). Catalog import uses NF-Standard JSON format: {vendor, system, profiles: [{code, type, uf, weight, ...}]}. Merged thermal-profiles returns builtin + vendor (source='vendor') + custom (source='custom') frames.
